script.openingLine: Welcome to Replay, where Spain and Argentina’s final unfolds again, moment by moment.

moments.spokenLine: The final begins: Spain meet Argentina at New York/New Jersey Stadium, with everything still to be decided.
moments.explainer: The replay opens with the two finalists meeting at the named stadium.

moments.spokenLine: At the interval, Spain and Argentina are level at 0–0, with neither side breaking through.
moments.explainer: The goalless score shows a tightly balanced first half.

moments.spokenLine: After ninety minutes, Spain and Argentina remain locked at 0–0. The World Cup final goes on.
moments.explainer: The deadlock carries the final beyond normal time.

moments.spokenLine: Argentina receive a confirmed red card, a major late turn in this final.
moments.explainer: The confirmed dismissal is a decisive change as extra time unfolds.

moments.spokenLine: Spain score, and the verified score moves to 1–0—the breakthrough this final had been waiting for.
moments.explainer: Spain finally break the 0–0 deadlock and take the lead.

moments.spokenLine: Argentina receive a confirmed yellow card as Spain hold their 1–0 lead.
moments.explainer: The card arrives with Spain still ahead on the verified score.

moments.spokenLine: A possible Spain goal is under review, with Spain leading 1–0.
moments.explainer: The review matters because another possible Spain goal could change the final score.

moments.spokenLine: The VAR review overturns the possible goal, and Spain remain 1–0 ahead.
moments.explainer: The score stays unchanged after the decision.

moments.spokenLine: Full time: Spain 1–0 Argentina. Spain have won the final.
moments.explainer: The final score confirms Spain’s victory.

chapters.text: At kick-off, The final begins: Spain meet Argentina at New York/New Jersey Stadium, with everything still to be decided.
At half-time, Spain and Argentina are level at 0–0, with neither side breaking through.
At the start of extra time, Spain and Argentina remain locked at 0–0. The World Cup final goes on.
In the 93rd minute, Argentina receive a confirmed red card, a major late turn in this final.
In the 106th minute, Spain score, and the verified score moves to 1–0—the breakthrough this final had been waiting for.
In the 112th minute, Argentina receive a confirmed yellow card as Spain hold their 1–0 lead.
In the 114th minute, A possible Spain goal is under review, with Spain leading 1–0.
In the 114th minute, The VAR review overturns the possible goal, and Spain remain 1–0 ahead.
At full-time, Spain 1–0 Argentina. Spain have won the final.
